_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, averaging the prices of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice and indexed to 2015 at 100, currently stands at 132, indicating a 32% rise since 2015. This increase is uneven across commodities and has been driven largely in recent years, reflecting volatility rather than a steady trend. The index highlights shifting supply-demand dynamics affecting these soft commodities globally. The climb underscores variability within the basket rather than a uniform price escalation.
notes.movers: Among the components, coffee prices have surged the most, up 93% since 2015, followed by orange juice with a 22% increase. Wheat and sugar have seen much smaller gains of 7% and 5%, respectively. These movements are closely linked to supply disruptions caused by adverse weather events and crop diseases—such as citrus greening and hurricanes affecting orange juice, and frost or drought impacting arabica coffee regions. The markets for these soft commodities tend to be thin, meaning limited supply or demand shifts can cause substantial price swings.
notes.why: This price behavior reflects how a few crops vulnerable to environmental factors can drive headline commodity indices substantially. Given the thinness of these markets and ongoing exposure to weather-related risks, elevated price levels may persist for extended periods. Such conditions highlight the structural challenges in stabilizing soft commodity prices globally.
